stem: A cloud computing server cluster uses 2 parallel nodes for redundancy. The request served requires at least one node to function. node 1 has a reliability between 74% and 86% (uncertain due to limited operational data). node 2 has a reliability of 89%. What is the overall probability that the request served?

ground_truth.answer: P(request served) ranges from 0.9714 to 0.9846. Point estimate: 0.978.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 2 nodes fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.26 × 0.11 = 0.0286. P(works) = 0.9714.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.14 × 0.11 = 0.0154. P(works) = 0.9846.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
